title: Function Composition and Pipe
description: Implement compose and pipe functions that take multiple functions as arguments and return a new function that is the composition of those functions. This exercise teaches functional programming concepts and higher-order functions.
instructions: Create a function named `compose` that takes multiple functions as arguments
Return a new function that applies the functions from right to left
Create a function named `pipe` that applies functions from left to right
Both should work with any number of functions
The composed function should pass the result of each function to the next
learningObjectives: Practice with reduceRight and reduce methods

testCases.name: Compose basic operations
testCases.setup: const add5 = x => x + 5; const multiply3 = x => x * 3; const subtract2 = x => x - 2; const fn = compose(subtract2, multiply3, add5);

testCases.name: Pipe basic operations
testCases.setup: const add5 = x => x + 5; const multiply3 = x => x * 3; const subtract2 = x => x - 2; const fn = pipe(add5, multiply3, subtract2);

testCases.name: Single function compose
testCases.setup: const double = x => x * 2; const fn = compose(double);

testCases.name: Single function pipe
testCases.setup: const double = x => x * 2; const fn = pipe(double);

testCases.name: Compose with strings
testCases.setup: const addExclamation = s => s + '!'; const toUpperCase = s => s.toUpperCase(); const fn = compose(addExclamation, toUpperCase);

testCases.name: Pipe order verification
testCases.setup: const square = x => x * x; const add10 = x => x + 10; const fn = pipe(square, add10);

testCases.name: Compose order verification
testCases.setup: const square = x => x * x; const add10 = x => x + 10; const fn = compose(square, add10);

hints.content: Both compose and pipe return a new function. The difference is the order: compose applies functions right-to-left, pipe applies left-to-right.
Use reduce for pipe (left-to-right) and reduceRight for compose (right-to-left). The accumulator is the result passed from one function to the next.
Pattern for compose: return (initialValue) => fns.reduceRight((acc, fn) => fn(acc), initialValue). For pipe, use reduce instead of reduceRight.
